hero_026.earlyGame: Since the Main Body is extremely fragile, it's essential to use the Puppet to secure last hits on minions from a safe distance and harass enemies. Until you reach Level 4 and unlock your Ultimate, your kill potential is low, so avoid forced damage trades and prioritize farming EXP and Gold safely.
hero_026.midGame: He truly shines from the mid-game once his skills are unlocked. Infiltrate the enemy jungle with the Puppet to secure vision and look for isolated enemy Mages or Marksmen. Wait for their core skills to be on cooldown, then swap the Puppet with the Main Body to quickly close the gap and unleash your burst damage.
hero_026.lateGame: He struggles in frontal teamfights, so he needs to play as an Assassin, flanking or attacking the enemy backline from behind. Use the Puppet to pressure enemy carries, and if anyone mispositions, quickly assassinate them and retreat. Use bushes cleverly to ensure your Main Body's position is never exposed.
hero_026.teamfight: Join the fight using your Puppet after your allied Tank engages. Restrain (Stun/Slow) the enemy with the Puppet's skills, swap positions with your Main Body, unleash your damage skills like your Ultimate, and strictly follow a 'hit-and-run' tactic to retreat immediately. Managing the Puppet's HP is also critical, as the Main Body will be stunned for a long time if the Puppet is destroyed.
hero_026.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is focusing too much on controlling the Puppet, leaving the defenseless Main Body exposed to be killed by enemies. Always hide the Main Body in a safe bush or under a tower. Also, since his combos are complex, it's easy to miscast a skill in a real match and accidentally expose the Main Body in the middle of the enemy formation, making combo practice in training mode essential.

hero_027.earlyGame: His base stats in the early game are high, and the knock-up from the second phase of Skill 1 is powerful. Take the initiative by actively trading damage in the Clash Lane. By using basic attacks while gaining a shield with Skill 2, you can deal true damage and gain the upper hand in the lane.
hero_027.midGame: Utilize your excellent CC (Crowd Control) and durability to group up with your allied Jungler or Mid Laner and force small skirmishes. The Ultimate (Skill 3) provides both long-range mobility and a stun, making him excellent at punishing poorly positioned enemies and initiating engagements.
hero_027.lateGame: Serve as the main tank by holding the frontline and absorbing enemy attacks. When your HP drops below 50%, your passive allows you to heal upon attacking, letting you hold the line to the bitter end. Peeling to protect your allied carries from enemy Assassins is also vital, so switch between offense and defense depending on the situation.
hero_027.teamfight: Initiate the fight by diving into the center of the enemy formation or targeting a troublesome backline carry with your Ultimate. Immediately use the knock-up from Skill 1 and the shield from Skill 2 to draw enemy aggro, creating an environment where your allies can deal damage safely. Even in chaotic fights, trust in your passive's healing and stubbornly keep using basic attacks and skills.
hero_027.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving too deep into the enemy formation alone with the Ultimate, leaving allies unable to follow up and getting isolated and killed. Always check your allies' positions before diving to ensure they are within follow-up range. Also, since the second phase knock-up requires landing the first phase of Skill 1, precise aiming is necessary.

hero_028.earlyGame: She excels at wave clearing because the water from Skill 2 bounces between minions and enemy heroes. Use minions to hit enemy heroes with Skill 2, stacking her passive freeze to apply pressure. Since she has low mobility, be extremely careful of the enemy Jungler hiding in the river bushes when looking to gank the side lanes.
hero_028.midGame: Her strengths shine from the mid-game when teamfights become more frequent. Always stick with your allied Tank or Roamer and continuously cast skills from a safe distance. If you can land Skill 2 bounces in crowded areas and follow up with the knock-up from Skill 1, you can freeze multiple enemies at once and turn the tide in your favor.
hero_028.lateGame: The massive AoE damage, slow, and freeze effects of her Ultimate are the keys to winning teamfights. Cast your Ultimate to engulf squishy targets like the enemy Marksman or Mage. However, since you will be a prime target for enemy Assassins, pay close attention to your positioning and never stray out of your allies' protection range.
hero_028.teamfight: The ideal combo is to drop your Ultimate on grouped-up enemies and overlap it with Skill 1 and Skill 2 to freeze the entire enemy team for a long duration. Skills are easier to land in narrow jungle paths or around the Overlord and Tyrant pits, so try to fight using the terrain to your advantage.
hero_028.commonMistakes: A fatal beginner mistake is getting too close to the enemy and entering the attack range of Assassins or Fighters. Always maintain maximum range during combat. Also, spamming skills without managing your passive freeze stacks (freezes at 3 stacks) means you won't maximize your CC potential, making stack management crucial.

hero_029.earlyGame: He excels at short trades thanks to the three consecutive dash attacks and knock-up from Skill 1. By utilizing the attack speed boost from his passive, you can weave basic attacks between skills to maximize damage. He lacks sustain until Level 4, so farm solidly while conserving HP.
hero_029.midGame: Once you unlock your Ultimate, you heal HP with every attack, making him unmatched in 1v1 duels. After taking down the Clash Lane tower, split-pushing to draw enemy attention or invading the enemy jungle to steal resources are effective strategies to bring an advantage to the whole team.
hero_029.lateGame: In teamfights, charging straight from the front will get you focused and killed before you can even heal. You must play like an Assassin, flanking from the side or rear to ambush the enemy backline (Mages and Marksmen) in sync with your team's engage. Save the CC cleanse effect of your Ultimate and cast it the moment you get hit by fatal CC to counterattack.
hero_029.teamfight: Close the gap with Skill 1 and stick to the enemy carry. The basic approach is to pop your Ultimate when your HP drops or when hit by CC, healing massively while wreaking havoc. The healing during his Ultimate is astonishing, so you will often need to confidently keep attacking rather than running away.
hero_029.commonMistakes: A common mistake is casting the Ultimate too early and wasting its healing effect, or casting it too late and dying before it triggers. It's best used when your HP drops to around half or the moment you get hit by hard CC. Also, failing to weave basic attacks between Skill 1 casts results in insufficient damage and missing passive stacks, so practice the rhythm.

hero_030.earlyGame: He is relatively weak in the early game because he hasn't accumulated passive stacks (which increase his attack power upon kills or assists). If jungling, aim to reach Level 4 quickly. If in the Clash Lane, avoid forced fights and clear minions under the tower. Safely securing assists during allied ganks is the fastest way to scale.
hero_030.midGame: Unlocking his Ultimate grants him true damage and a powerful slow, drastically spiking his kill potential. Actively hunt isolated enemies using the combo: close the gap (or cleanse CC) with Skill 1 → ambush with Ultimate → boost attack speed and lifesteal with Skill 2 to win the duel, steadily racking up kills and passive stacks.
hero_030.lateGame: Once he reaches max passive stacks (20 stacks), he gains an incredible amount of physical attack. A full-build Dian Wei boasts enough firepower to melt even Tanks in seconds. However, due to his low mobility, he shouldn't charge head-on in teamfights. Ideally, wait until the enemy's CC skills are exhausted before joining the fray to clean up the remaining enemies.
hero_030.teamfight: It is essential to hide in a bush and ambush the enemy backline, or follow up from the rear after an allied engage. Dive the enemy carry with your Ultimate, and instantly crush them using the explosive attack speed and lifesteal from Skill 2. If hit by CC, stay calm, use Skill 1 to cleanse it, and decide whether to pursue or retreat.
hero_030.commonMistakes: A typical beginner mistake is wasting Skill 1's CC cleanse just for the movement speed, leaving him unable to cleanse a crucial stun and getting killed. If the enemy has heavy CC, playstyle demands saving Skill 1 exclusively for cleansing. Also, recklessly challenging 1v1s in the weak early game is a major mistake.

hero_031.earlyGame: By managing her passive 'Momentum' stacks, her basic attacks transform into powerful effects (dash, heal, execute). Her early jungle clear speed is average, but once she unlocks her Ultimate at Level 4, she becomes a potent ganker. Utilize the targeted knock-up and anti-heal from her Ultimate to gank lanes where you can secure a kill.
hero_031.midGame: She has a huge advantage against Mages and Marksmen since she can neutralize enemy projectiles with Skill 1. In skirmishes, use Skill 1 to erase key enemy skills, and chase them down with the shield and dash from Skill 2. If an enemy carry is isolated, finish them off with your Ultimate to secure objectives.
hero_031.lateGame: In teamfights, positioning after diving in with your Ultimate is crucial. Recklessly charging in will draw focused fire, so wait for your allied Tank to absorb some skills, then cast your Ultimate on the enemy's most dangerous damage dealer. Destroy the enemy backline with sudden burst damage and sustained combat power from her 'Momentum'.
hero_031.teamfight: Dive in by targeting the enemy carry with your Ultimate, knocking them airborne and applying anti-heal. Upon landing, quickly finish them off by alternating 'Momentum' empowered basic attacks and skills. The ideal scenario is negating enemy counterattacks (especially strong projectiles) with well-timed Skill 1 wind blades, going on a rampage while mitigating damage.
hero_031.commonMistakes: Using another skill without weaving a basic attack and wasting the passive empowered basic attack ('Momentum' stack) is a huge loss. You must drill the 'Skill → Empowered Basic Attack → Skill' rhythm into your muscle memory. Furthermore, treating Skill 1 purely as a damage skill instead of using it to negate projectiles is a waste of its potential.

hero_032.earlyGame: His early jungle clear is relatively slow and he is vulnerable to enemy jungle invades, so aim for Level 4 safely with the help of your allies. His Ultimate requires 4 basic attacks to unlock, so when ganking, you need to creatively build stacks on jungle monsters or minions before heading to the lane.
hero_032.midGame: His signature hit-and-run tactic is perfected here: using the double dash and return from Skill 1, along with the untargetability and defense reduction of Skill 2, to deal damage gracefully and return unscathed. Build stacks on jungle monsters, dive the enemy with Skill 1, unleash Skill 2 and Ultimate, and return to your original position to continuously poke down the enemy's HP.
hero_032.lateGame: Li Bai's hit-and-run is overwhelmingly powerful during tower sieges or objective standoffs. However, Li Bai himself is extremely squishy, and if enemies ambush his return point (the shadow from Skill 1), he will be easily killed. It's crucial to approach from various angles so enemies can't predict your return position.
hero_032.teamfight: Avoid engaging head-on; instead, use nearby minions or monsters to unlock your Ultimate, dash into the enemy backline, deal massive damage, and retreat. Skill 2 and the Ultimate render you untargetable (invincible) during cast, requiring high player skill to use these to dodge fatal enemy damage and CC.
hero_032.commonMistakes: A very common mistake is diving into the enemy formation with Skill 1 only to realize the Ultimate isn't unlocked, resulting in a swift death. Always check your stack status before dashing. Another typical beginner mistake is blindly returning to your shadow when enemies are clearly camping it, resulting in a free kill.

hero_033.earlyGame: Harassing with Skill 1's continuous shooting is strong, but it can't pierce minions, so positioning is key. Since he relies on Energy rather than Mana, landing Skill 1 to restore Energy is crucial. His early game dueling is weak, so focus purely on farming from a safe distance.
hero_033.midGame: Once his attack speed hits certain thresholds (like 130%), the bullet count of his Skill 1 and Ultimate increases, causing a massive spike in damage. As your core items are completed in the mid-game, use your mobility to group up and actively participate in objective fights. Racking up passive stacks by hitting enemies with basic attacks and skills to trigger true damage is the core of his firepower.
hero_033.lateGame: Thanks to Skill 2 (blink) and his passive (movement speed boost when enemies are nearby), he possesses extremely high survivability and kiting potential for a Marksman. Dodge enemy Assassin and Fighter engages with Skill 2 while poking them down with Skill 1. When enemy HP is low, look to wipe them out completely with your Ultimate.
hero_033.teamfight: Never step forward at the start of a teamfight. Stay behind your Tank, poke the enemy frontline with Skill 1, and build up your passive stacks (defense break/true damage). At the decisive moment when the enemy's major CC is on cooldown and stacks are applied across the enemy team, dive right into the center with your Ultimate (devastating when paired with the Stun summoner spell) to deal massive AoE damage.
hero_033.commonMistakes: Despite being a Marksman, diving into the enemy team with your Ultimate at the start of a fight and getting instantly blown up by CC is something to strictly avoid. Marco Polo's Ultimate is an 'execution' skill. Also, missing Skill 1 repeatedly will drain your Energy, leaving you helpless, so improving your aim is a must.

hero_034.earlyGame: Thanks to his passive, his attack speed and movement speed increase with every basic attack. By maintaining these stacks during lane phase, he can output very high DPS right from the start. Use your piercing empowered basic attacks through minions to safely poke the enemy Marksman and take control of the lane.
hero_034.midGame: Because Skill 2 offers a CC cleanse and brief invincibility, he has far better self-peel than other Marksmen. Even if ganked by the enemy Jungler, he can cleanse slows or stuns with Skill 2 and survive. He excels at taking down towers, so once you destroy your lane's tower, rotate to the Mid Lane to accelerate the game's pace.
hero_034.lateGame: Once fully built, his passive combined with items gives him insane attack speed, making his kiting unstoppable. Landing your Ultimate (yellow card) on the enemy frontline (like a Tank) before a teamfight drastically reduces their physical and magical defense (by 50%), allowing you to easily melt even the tankiest enemies.
hero_034.teamfight: Always maintain maximum range and continuously land basic attacks on whichever enemy gets closest to you. Use your Ultimate either defensively to stop enemy engages or aggressively on a locked-down target to shred their defense. If an enemy Assassin jumps on you, stay calm, cleanse the CC with Skill 2, reposition with Flash, and counterattack.
hero_034.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is wasting Skill 2 as a simple damage tool, leaving you unable to cleanse a crucial CC from an enemy Assassin or Mage when it matters most, resulting in your death. Always save Skill 2 strictly for 'cleansing enemy CC'. Additionally, initiating combat without maintaining passive stacks will lower your damage, so practice stacking them on thin air beforehand.

hero_035.earlyGame: He possesses a passive that heals HP and boosts physical defense with empowered basic attacks after using skills, giving him excellent dueling and lane sustain. Capitalize on the dash and knock-up from Skill 1 to dominate short trades. He lacks kill pressure before Level 4, so refrain from reckless tower dives and focus on farming.
hero_035.midGame: Unlocking his Ultimate allows for a brutal combo where he kicks enemies into walls to stun them. He thrives in small skirmishes in tight spaces with many walls, such as the jungle or near towers. Actively perform 'bush kills' by hiding in a bush and ambushing approaching enemies by kicking them into a wall for an instant kill.
hero_035.lateGame: Landing your Ultimate on the enemy backline dictates the outcome of teamfights. Use a long-range engage combining Flash and Skill 1 to unexpectedly kick the enemy Marksman or Mage into a wall. However, missing your Ultimate drastically reduces your damage and leaves you isolated deep in enemy territory, making your engagement timing absolutely crucial.
hero_035.teamfight: Instead of charging straight in, flank the enemy backline from side bushes. The ultimate setup is using 'Skill 1 → Flash' to close the gap and kicking multiple enemies into a wall with your Ultimate. A successful wall-kick unlocks the second phase of his Ultimate (a flying kick dealing percentage missing HP damage), which, paired with the defense reduction from Skill 2, will instantly obliterate the enemy team.
hero_035.commonMistakes: A classic beginner mistake is using the Ultimate in an open area, simply knocking enemies far away without stunning them and accidentally saving them. Always be conscious of 'kicking enemies toward a wall' when using Dharma's Ultimate. Also, sloppy aiming when kicking can knock enemies right out of your allies' skill range, so aim carefully.

hero_036.earlyGame: He boasts incredibly high durability thanks to his passive, which significantly reduces damage taken when his HP drops below 30%. The dash and knockback from Skill 1 are perfect for controlling minion waves or disrupting enemies. When playing as a Roamer, your primary role is defensive—preventing enemy jungle invades and protecting your Marksman.
hero_036.midGame: Serve as the team's shield on the frontline by utilizing your excellent engage and peel capabilities. When you spot poorly positioned enemies, push them toward your allies with Skill 1, apply the slow and damage reduction from Skill 2, and assist in securing the kill. Conversely, when allies are targeted, push enemies away to buy time for their escape.
hero_036.lateGame: His Ultimate deals higher damage the lower his HP gets and grants temporary invincibility (or powerful damage reduction) upon casting. By enduring the enemy's fierce onslaught in teamfights and unleashing your Ultimate at the very last second when your HP is critically low, you can deal devastating AoE burst damage despite being a Tank.
hero_036.teamfight: Your top priority is protecting your team's main carry. If an enemy Assassin or Fighter dives in, immediately push them away with Skill 1 and neutralize their firepower with Skill 2. When switching to offense, use the 'Flash + Skill 1' combo for a surprise engage. Once the enemy formation is broken, slam your Ultimate to seal the victory.
hero_036.commonMistakes: A frequent 'friendly fire' mistake is using Skill 1 to push a fleeing, low-HP enemy to safety, preventing your allies from finishing them off. Always aim to push enemies 'toward your allies' or 'into a wall'. Also, when his passive damage reduction is on cooldown, he is surprisingly squishy, so be careful not to overextend with misplaced confidence.

hero_037.earlyGame: Clear the jungle quickly right from the start, and look to snowball by executing a gank the moment you reach Level 4. While managing your Energy carefully, utilize your high mobility to punish any enemies stepping out of position.
hero_037.midGame: Once you build some Cooldown Reduction items, you'll be able to cycle your skills continuously. Don't miss any isolated enemy Mages or Marksmen; instantly unleash your burst damage to rack up kills and seize map control.
hero_037.lateGame: As enemies start to group up, Sima Yi, being an Assassin, will find it harder to maneuver. Rather than initiating the fight yourself, play the role of the cleanup crew: wait for your team to bait out heavy enemy CC, then dive the enemy backline with your Ultimate to decimate their squishies.
hero_037.teamfight: Positioning to flank from the side or behind is vital in teamfights. Dive the enemy carry with your Ultimate, silence them with Skill 2 to prevent counterattacks, instantly delete them with your empowered basic attacks, and immediately retreat using Skill 1 in a classic hit-and-run style.
hero_037.commonMistakes: A common beginner mistake is charging head-on into a teamfight, only to get hit by CC and killed without doing anything. Additionally, since his damage relies almost entirely on the empowered basic attacks following his skills, you must remain calm and master the 'Skill → Basic Attack' combo rhythm.

hero_038.earlyGame: In the Clash Lane early game, managing your passive gauge is everything. Engaging with a full gauge is incredibly powerful, but you are very weak without it. Attack minions to build up your gauge, and only trade damage when the timing favors you.
hero_038.midGame: His unmatched 1v1 dueling prowess makes him ideal for split-pushing deep into the side lanes. Even if the enemy Jungler comes to gank, you can tie one of them down with your Ultimate, endure the assault using the damage reduction from Skill 2, and turn the tables on them.
hero_038.lateGame: Once fully built, his tower-taking speed becomes absurd. Push the lane opposite to the objective (Tyrant or Overlord) your team is taking, forcing the enemy into a macro dilemma: 'Do we defend the tower, or contest the objective?' thereby heavily contributing to the team.
hero_038.teamfight: You don't need to force yourself into 5v5 teamfights, but if you do group up, your role is to use Flash into your Ultimate to forcibly lock down the enemy carry (Mage or Marksman) and secure a guaranteed kill. Cast Skill 2 right as the enemy unleashes their burst damage to minimize the damage you take.
hero_038.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is wasting Skill 2 (damage reduction and reflection) casually. If you don't time it against the enemy's most damaging skills or basic attacks, you'll just be a sitting duck. Furthermore, prioritizing teamfights over split-pushing prevents you from fully capitalizing on his strengths.

hero_039.earlyGame: Dominate the lane by maintaining your passive's charge state (which boosts your movement speed). Stopping drastically drops your damage, so keep your movement joystick moving constantly. A highly effective strategy is rotating to the Mid Lane to assist with a gank by knocking enemies back from your charge state.
hero_039.midGame: Utilize your high mobility to roam the entire map, applying continuous pressure to every lane. Even when enemies are pushed under their own tower, you can secure kills by flanking behind them and pushing them out toward your team (an 'Insec' play).
hero_039.lateGame: Once your Cooldown Reduction reaches its cap, you can spam knockbacks almost infinitely while your Ultimate is active. Just before a teamfight, disappear from enemy vision, then charge in from the flank or rear at the perfect moment to completely shatter their formation.
hero_039.teamfight: Charging straight in from the front is strictly prohibited. Because any CC (slow or stun) will break your charge state, wait until your team starts the fight before diving in from their blind spots. Push the enemy carries to isolate them, and use the cleanse from Skill 2 to keep rampaging without being stopped.
hero_039.commonMistakes: Panicking and lifting your finger off the movement joystick, causing your charge state to drop, is a fatal error. Another common beginner mistake is charging in while the enemy still has their heavy CC skills available, leading to an instant death. You must carefully judge the perfect timing to use the Summoner Spells 'Purify' or 'Sprint'.

hero_040.earlyGame: She is weak early on due to high mana consumption, so avoid forced fights and focus purely on clearing minions. Farm safely using Skill 1 and Skill 2. Until your first core item (like Insatiable Tome) is complete, prioritize supporting your Jungler over aggressive plays.
hero_040.midGame: Once she gets her spell vamp items and some Cooldown Reduction, she becomes unmatched in small skirmishes. A powerful tactic is to continuously dodge enemy skills with the invincibility frames of Skill 2 while spreading your passive's true damage and healing to slowly whittle down the enemy's health.
hero_040.lateGame: A full-build Diaochan boasts terrifying sustain (continuous combat ability) in teamfights. If you can keep dodging focused fire with Skill 2, she has the potential to win 1vX scenarios. However, the enemy will also build anti-heal items, so overconfidence can be deadly.
hero_040.teamfight: Never initiate the fight yourself. Wait to confirm that the enemy has blown their major hard CC (especially un-cleansable ones like Suppression) on your Tanks, then enter the fight by laying down your Ultimate's circle. Inside the circle, quickly loop Skill 1 and Skill 2, dealing continuous damage while 'dancing' to dodge enemy attacks.
hero_040.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is using Skill 2 purely to dash straight at the enemy. If you don't actively use the brief invincibility frames during the dash to 'dodge enemy damage and CC', you'll just be a sitting duck. Additionally, fighting outside your Ultimate's circle severely drops your damage output, so positional awareness is key.

hero_041.earlyGame: She is extremely weak before reaching Level 4 and unlocking her Ultimate, so farm as fast as possible while staying alert for enemy jungle invades. Once at Level 4, apply marks with Skill 1 and Skill 2, and execute continuous dash ganks by resetting your Ultimate to widen the gold gap.
hero_041.midGame: When her core items are complete, she exhibits unstoppable dominance in mid-sized teamfights. Look for outplays by using minions or neutral monsters as stepping stones to apply marks, quickly closing the distance to the enemy carry from afar, and bursting them down.
hero_041.lateGame: Since enemy damage scales up and CC becomes abundant, a single mistake in resetting your Ultimate can lead to instant death. It is crucial to build defensive items like 'Sage's Sanctuary' (Resurrection Armor) or 'Splendor' (Stasis item), and strictly adhere to hit-and-run tactics to boost your survivability.
hero_041.teamfight: Charging head-on will invite focused fire, so wait in the flanking bushes for the right moment during teamfights. The second your allies engage and enemy CC is exhausted, dive in, spread marks across multiple enemies, and wreak havoc on their formation by darting around with your Ultimate.
hero_041.commonMistakes: The fatal mistake is casting the Ultimate on an unmarked enemy, causing it to go on cooldown (commonly known as 'breaking the Ult'). Avoid mashing buttons in a panic; you must build the muscle memory in training mode to only swipe your Ultimate after confirming a mark is present.

hero_042.earlyGame: Due to low durability and mobility in the early game, focus on clearing minions from safe positions, like under your tower, using Skill 1 and Skill 2. To ensure you collect the EXP orbs from your passive, make it a habit to deal damage (tag them) with your skills right before enemy heroes or minions die.
hero_042.midGame: If you've been collecting EXP efficiently, you will hit Level 15 faster than anyone else and unlock 'Apotheosis', allowing you to uncap one ally's max level to 25. Grant this divine power to the ally with the highest carry potential (a fed Marksman or Jungler) to instantly create a massive stat advantage.
hero_042.lateGame: Because your own level cap is also unlocked, you can overwhelm enemies through pure stats. He excels in objective (Tyrant and Overlord) standoffs as an artillery Mage, landing devastating defense-shredding debuffs and slows from afar with Skill 1, and chunking enemy health with charged Ultimate attacks.
hero_042.teamfight: Always maintain the safest possible position behind your frontline. Halt enemy movements with the knock-up from Skill 2, apply continuous damage and debuffs with Skill 1, and align your Ultimate to unleash massive damage. If targeted by an Assassin, do not hesitate to use Flash to create distance.
hero_042.commonMistakes: Stepping too far forward, getting caught by enemy Assassins or Fighters, and dying helplessly is the classic mistake. Furthermore, carelessly using 'Apotheosis' on an underperforming ally or the Support completely wastes the strength of his passive, so choose your target wisely.

hero_043.earlyGame: Played as either a Clash Laner or Roamer (Support). In the laning phase, pop the shield from Skill 1 and detonate it at the right time for favorable damage trades. The moment you hit Level 4, check the status of other lanes and be ready to instantly teleport with your Ultimate when an ally is in danger or initiating a gank.
hero_043.midGame: Focus on split-pushing (pushing side lanes alone) to draw enemy attention. The moment enemies come to defend, use your Ultimate to teleport to your allies fighting on the other side of the map. This macro play creates a numerical advantage, allowing your team to win teamfights and secure objectives.
hero_043.lateGame: As the enemy carries scale in damage, your top priority becomes defending your allied Marksman or Mage with your life. Anticipate when the enemy Assassin will dive in, cast your Ultimate on your allied carry to grant them a massive shield, protect them, and then seamlessly transition into a counterattack to suppress the enemy.
hero_043.teamfight: After joining your allies with your Ultimate, disrupt the enemy formation with the dash-stun from Skill 2, or peel for your allied carry. The shield from Skill 1 isn't just for durability; it's also a source of burst damage, so try to detonate it right in the middle of clustered enemies.
hero_043.commonMistakes: The worst mistake is being too stingy with your Ultimate or casting it too late, resulting in 'arriving late' after your ally has already died. You must constantly monitor the minimap and allied health bars, developing the judgment to start channeling your Ultimate early when a fight seems imminent.

hero_044.earlyGame: Utilize your incredibly high mobility to clear your jungle as fast as possible and hit Level 4. Afterward, exploit enemy openings to gank or invade the enemy jungle to steal buffs. Sprint across the entire map to widen the gold advantage right from the early game.
hero_044.midGame: Avoid direct 5v5 clashes and always opt for guerrilla warfare to annoy the enemy. Push unguarded side lane towers alone, and when multiple enemies come to defend, safely escape using your numerous dash skills. This macro strategy forces enemies out of position, giving your team free rein to take objectives on the opposite side of the map.
hero_044.lateGame: As enemy damage peaks in the late game, Han Xin's low durability means he'll melt instantly. Never initiate a teamfight. Instead, act strictly as a 'sweeper' who enters late to execute low-health enemies, or ignore the teamfight entirely to sneakily perform a 'backdoor' and destroy the enemy's base towers or crystal.
hero_044.teamfight: Wait in a blind spot (like a bush) and watch until the enemy's CC (crowd control) and high-damage skills are blown. Dive the enemy backline (Marksman or Mage) with 'Skill 2 → Skill 1', endure their counterattacks with the Super Armor (CC immunity) from your Ultimate while dealing burst damage, and then use your remaining skills to escape.
hero_044.commonMistakes: Overestimating his mobility and diving into disadvantageous 1v3 situations, only to be chain-CC'd and killed, is a common rookie death. Also, getting too focused on securing kills and neglecting 'objective management' like pushing towers or taking dragons will prevent you from utilizing Han Xin's true strengths and lead to defeat.

hero_045.earlyGame: In the Mid Lane, use Skill 1 and your empowered basic attacks to clear minions quickly and safely. At Level 2, leverage the shield from your passive and the harass from Skill 1 to pressure the enemy Mage. Once you gain lane priority, roam to support your side lanes or Jungler by providing vision.
hero_045.midGame: In small skirmishes, her CC (crowd control) is lethal. By following up your allies' CC (like a Tank's knock-up or stun) with the freeze from your Skill 2, you can guarantee a complete lockdown on the enemy. When contesting objectives (Tyrant or Overlord), casting your Ultimate in chokepoints to zone out approaching enemies is highly effective.
hero_045.lateGame: Once Cooldown Reduction items are built, the uptime on your Ultimate becomes very high. Don't hesitate to use it aggressively to clear minion waves or split the enemy formation. The outcome of a teamfight entirely depends on whether you can land the freeze from Skill 2 on the enemy's key players (carries).
hero_045.teamfight: The basic combo is casting Skill 1 from the backline to slow enemies and restrict their movement, making it easier to land the freeze with Skill 2. If the freeze connects, instantly drop your Ultimate to inflict massive damage. If an enemy Assassin jumps on you, place Skill 2 or your Ultimate directly at your feet and Flash away to defend yourself while counterattacking.
hero_045.commonMistakes: Casting Skill 2 (freeze) directly at the enemy's current position is a mistake, as the casting delay allows them to easily walk out of it. You must develop the habit of 'leading your target' by predicting their movement, or consistently applying the slow from Skill 1 first; otherwise, you'll be a Mage whose CC never lands.

hero_046.earlyGame: Take full advantage of the privilege of being able to turn invisible (stealth) from Level 1 to clear the jungle quickly and gank without being noticed. Because your early-game burst damage is incredibly high, your primary job is to target squishy Marksmen and Mages for guaranteed kills to snowball the game.
hero_046.midGame: Roam the map relentlessly, hunting for any isolated enemy carries trying to farm. Although a warning mark appears over their heads when you approach in stealth, you can use this to your advantage in a psychological war: 'exert pressure without revealing yourself, forcing enemies under their towers so your team can freely take objectives'.
hero_046.lateGame: As enemies build defensive items and group up, Lanling King starts to fall off. Instead of forcing solo kills, transition your playstyle: use your stealth to act as a 'mobile ward' to secure enemy vision, and capitalize on the chaos of teamfights to assassinate low-health stragglers.
hero_046.teamfight: Charging in from the front will get you instantly focused and melted. Never reveal yourself before the teamfight truly begins. Wait for your allied Tanks to absorb the enemy's CC, and once the fight turns into a chaotic brawl, flank the enemy backline from the side or rear, unleash your 'Skill 2 → Ultimate → Skill 1' combo, and immediately disengage.
hero_046.commonMistakes: Getting too close to enemies carelessly while in stealth, triggering the warning mark and exposing your position, only to get caught in AoE damage or random CC and revealed, is fatal. Also, missing Skill 2 (the dagger) but still blindly diving in with your Ultimate, resulting in insufficient damage and getting killed in retaliation, is a very common blunder.
